post_state_files.client.py:
from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

import httpx


class APIError(Exception):
    """Raised when the remote API returns a 4xx or 5xx status."""
    def __init__(self, status_code: int, body: Optional[str] = None):
        super().__init__(f'API error {status_code}: {body}')
        self.status_code = status_code
        self.body = body


class APIClient:
    """Async API client. Wraps httpx.AsyncClient."""

    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: float = 5.0):
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
        self.timeout = timeout

    async def fetch_user(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """GET /users/{user_id} → JSON dict. Raises APIError on 4xx/5xx,
        re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/users/{user_id}')
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def create_post(self, author_id: int, title: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """POST /posts. Body must be {author_id, title}. Returns the created post."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.post(
                f'{self.base_url}/posts',
                json={'author_id': author_id, 'title': title},
            )
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def ping(self) -> bool:
        """GET /healthz → True iff response is 200."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            try:
                r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/healthz')
                return r.status_code == 200
            except httpx.TransportError:
                return False
post_state_files.test_client.py:
"""Tests for the async API client in client.py."""
from typing import Any, Dict
import httpx
import pytest
from client import APIClient, APIError


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_success(httpx_mock):
    """Test successful fetch_user returns JSON dict."""
    user_data = {"id": 1, "name": "Alice"}
    httpx_mock.add_response(
        url="http://example.com/users/1",
        json=user_data,
        status_code=200,
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    result = await client.fetch_user(1)
    assert result == user_data


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_404_raises_api_error(httpx_mock):
    """Test fetch_user raises APIError on 404."""
    httpx_mock.add_response(
        url="http://example.com/users/999",
        status_code=404,
        text="Not Found",
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
        await client.fetch_user(999)
    assert exc_info.value.status_code == 404
    assert exc_info.value.body == "Not Found"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_fetch_user_network_error(httpx_mock):
    """Test fetch_user re-raises TransportError on network failure."""
    httpx_mock.add_exception(
        httpx.TransportError("Connection refused"),
        url="http://example.com/users/1",
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    with pytest.raises(httpx.TransportError):
        await client.fetch_user(1)


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post_success(httpx_mock):
    """Test successful create_post returns created post."""
    post_data = {"id": 10, "author_id": 1, "title": "Hello"}
    httpx_mock.add_response(
        url="http://example.com/posts",
        method="POST",
        json=post_data,
        status_code=201,
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    result = await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="Hello")
    assert result == post_data


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post_400_raises_api_error(httpx_mock):
    """Test create_post raises APIError on 400."""
    httpx_mock.add_response(
        url="http://example.com/posts",
        method="POST",
        status_code=400,
        text="Bad Request",
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
        await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="")
    assert exc_info.value.status_code == 400
    assert exc_info.value.body == "Bad Request"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post_network_error(httpx_mock):
    """Test create_post re-raises TransportError on network failure."""
    httpx_mock.add_exception(
        httpx.TransportError("Connection refused"),
        url="http://example.com/posts",
        method="POST",
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    with pytest.raises(httpx.TransportError):
        await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="Hello")


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_200_returns_true(httpx_mock):
    """Test ping returns True when healthz returns 200."""
    httpx_mock.add_response(
        url="http://example.com/healthz",
        status_code=200,
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    result = await client.ping()
    assert result is True


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_non_200_returns_false(httpx_mock):
    """Test ping returns False when healthz returns non-200."""
    httpx_mock.add_response(
        url="http://example.com/healthz",
        status_code=503,
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    result = await client.ping()
    assert result is False


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ping_network_error_returns_false(httpx_mock):
    """Test ping returns False on network error (does not raise)."""
    httpx_mock.add_exception(
        httpx.TransportError("Connection refused"),
        url="http://example.com/healthz",
    )
    client = APIClient(base_url="http://example.com")
    result = await client.ping()
    assert result is False
